```{r allData, echo = F}
DICT <- cbind.data.frame(var = c("pco_1", "pco_9", "pco_7", "pco_11", 
                       "pco_3", "adult_body_length_mm", "max_longevity_d", "pco_12", "interbirth_interval_d", "habitat_breadth_n", "weaning_age_d",  "generation_length_d", "maturity_d", "pco_2", "bio_afrotropical", "female_maturity_d", "bio_indomalayan", "gestation_length_d", "brain_mass_g", "neonate_mass_g", "pco_8", "pco_4", "det_fruit", "adult_mass_g", "bio_nearctic", "pco_10", "det_inv", "pco_5", "age_first_reproduction_d", "pco_6", "dphy_plant", "litters_per_year_n", "dphy_invertebrate", "iucn_shrubland", "litter_size_n", "fossoriality", "iucn_artificialterr", "dphy_vertebrate", "det_plantother", "det_vfish", "hibernation_torpor", "terrestrial_non.volant", "iucn_savanna", "foraging_arboreal", "bio_palearctic", "det_vend", "foraging_aerial", "iucn_caves", "bio_neotropical", "terrestrial_volant", "iucn_marinecoastal", "trophic_level", "foraging_ground", "activity_cycle", "iucn_grassland", "det_diet_breadth_n", "iucn_forest", "det_vect", "det_seed", "iucn_desert", "island_mainland", "iucn_wetlands", "det_scav", "bio_australasian", "det_nect", "marine", "iucn_marineneritic", "freshwater", "island_endemic", "det_vunk", "iucn_rocky", "foraging_marine", "iucn_marineintertidal", "bio_antarctic", "iucn_marineoceanic", "island_landbridge", "island_marine", "foraging_scansorial", "bio_oceanian", "iucn_artificialaquatic", "iucn_marinedeep", "iucn_introducedveg", "adult_forearm_length_mm", "disected_by_mountains", "island_dwelling", "pco_15", "pco_16", "pco_18", "pco_13", "pco_17", "pco_14", "min_aridity_index", "mean_aridity_index", "perc80_aridity_index", "relative_wing_loading", "aspect_ratio", "forearm_mm", "migration", "mixed_species_association_n", "cave_roost", "cavity_roost", "foliage_roost", "generalist_roost", "iucn_moistforest", "iucn_moistsavanna", "relative_age_first_birth", "female_relative_maturity", "median_aggregation_size", "lower_elevation_m", "mass_specific_production_rate", "upper_elevation_m"),
                       type = c("phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "size", "reproduction", "phylogenetic", "reproduction", "habitat", "reproduction", "reproduction", "reproduction", "phylogenetic", "geography", "reproduction", "geography", "reproduction", "size", "size", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "diet", "size", "geography", "phylogenetic", "diet", "phylogenetic", "reproduction", "phylogenetic", "diet", "reproduction", "diet", "habitat", "reproduction", "movement", "habitat", "diet", "diet", "diet", "movement", "movement", "habitat", "movement", "geography", "diet", "movement", "habitat", "geography", "movement", "habitat", "diet", "movement", "movement", "habitat", "diet", "habitat", "diet", "diet", "habitat", "geography", "habitat", "diet", "geography", "diet", "movement", "habitat", "movement", "geography", "diet", "habitat", "movement", "habitat", "geography", "habitat", "geography", "geography", "movement", "geography",  "habitat", "habitat", "habitat", "size", "geography", "geography", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "phylogenetic", "geography", "geography", "geography", "size", "size", "size", "movement", "reproduction", "habitat", "habitat", "habitat", "habitat", "habitat", "habitat", "reproduction", "reproduction", "size", "habitat", "reproduction", "habitat"))
COLS <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
COLS <- cbind.data.frame(value = rev(COLS[1:7]),
                         name = c("diet", "geography", "habitat", "movement", "phylogenetic", "reproduction", "size"))
PCOA <- read.csv("batpcoa.csv")
TREE <- read.beast("~/Downloads/MamPhy_fullPosterior_BDvr_Completed_5911sp_topoCons_FBDasZhouEtAl_MCC_v2_target.tre")
ORDS <- sapply(strsplit(TREE@phylo$tip.label, "_"), function(x) unlist(x)[4]) #The order is listed as the fourth object after a strsplit of "_", so we can grab this and subset to only carnivores
TREE <- drop.tip(TREE@phylo, which(!ORDS %in% c("CHIROPTERA"))) 
NAME <- cbind.data.frame(family = sapply(strsplit(TREE$tip.label, "_"), function(x) unlist(x)[3]),
                 species = sapply(strsplit(TREE$tip.label, "_"), function(i) paste(i[1], i[2], collapse = " ")))
balt <- PCOA[-which(duplicated(PCOA$tree_match)), ]
TREE$tip.label <- NAME$species
NAME %<>%
  filter(species %in% balt$tree_match)
TREE <- drop.tip(TREE, TREE$tip.label[!TREE$tip.label %in% balt$tree_match]) 
PLOS <- read.csv("PredictedBatsHan2016.csv") %>%
  mutate(species = gsub("_", " ", species)) %>%
  mutate(label = case_when(label != "1" ~ 0,
                           label == "1" ~ 1))
PLOS$species[PLOS$species %in% c("Artibeus glaucus", "Myotis ricketti", "Neoromicia nanus", "Vampyressa bidens")] <- c("Myotis pilosus", "Dermanura glauca", "Neoromicia nana", "Vampyriscus bidens")
# GORK <- read_xlsx("renpc1discussion/1_3_bats_obs_pred.xlsx", sheet = 2) %>%
#   janitor::clean_names() %>%
#   dplyr::select(species, ebov_auc, bomv_auc, bdbv_auc, tafv_auc, restv_auc, sudv_auc, mlav_auc, llov_auc, marv_auc, ravn_auc)
#TAB <- read_xlsx("Table_S4.xlsx", sheet = 6)
#GORK %<>%
#  mutate(dehv_auc = TAB$`DEHV AUC`[match(GORK$species, TAB$Species)])
#GORK[which(is.na(GORK$dehv_auc)), "dehv_auc"] <- c(225.426976472792, 134.968000764585, NA, NA)
GORK <- read.csv("bats_obs_pred.csv")
RANG <- apply(GORK[, -1], 2, max, na.rm = T) - apply(GORK[, -1], 2, min, na.rm = T)
RANG <- RANG[c(1, 3, 2, 4:5, 9, 8, 6, 7, 10, 11)]
```

# All {.tabset}

## Summary Figures {.tabset}

### Bat PCo Summary
```{r batpcosummary1, fig.width=10, fig.height=12, message = F, warning=F, fig.cap="A summary of how the principal coordinate axes relate to larger bat taxonomy. Each panel in a column depicts a bat family with their values highlighted for a particular PCo axis. Warmer colors represent higher values and darker colors represent lower values. Note that these axis values do not carry inherent value but are useful for comparison"}
PCOA %<>%
  mutate(genus = sapply(strsplit(tree_match, " "), "[", 1))
GENS <- read.csv("FilovirusPredictions.csv") %>%
  mutate(genus = sapply(strsplit(species, " "), "[", 1)) %>%
  distinct(family, genus)
PCOA %<>%
  mutate(family = GENS[match(PCOA$genus, GENS$genus), "family"])
#which(is.na(match(PCOA$genus, GENS$genus)))
PCOA[c(458, 468), "family"] <- "Phyllostomidae"
PCOV <- reshape2::melt(PCOA)
PCOV <- lapply(unique(PCOV$variable), function(i) PCOV[PCOV$variable == i, ])
ANAM <- PCOA %>%
  filter(family %in% c("Emballonuridae", "Hipposideridae", "Molossidae", "Miniopteridae", "Pteropodidae", "Rhinolophidae", "Phyllostomidae", "Vespertilionidae")) %>%
  arrange(family) %$%
  tree_match
FPLT <- foreach(i = 1:17) %do% {
  PCOV[[i]] %>%
  filter(family %in% c("Emballonuridae", "Hipposideridae", "Molossidae", "Miniopteridae", "Pteropodidae", "Rhinolophidae", "Phyllostomidae", "Vespertilionidae")) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           aes(x = tree_match,
               y = value,
               color = value,
               fill = value),
           show.legend = T) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B",
                         limits = c(-75, 38.5),
                         breaks = seq(-75, 35, 10)) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "B",
                         limits = c(-75, 38.5),
                         seq(-75, 35, 10)) +
  gghighlight(use_direct_label = F) +
  facet_grid(variable ~ family, scales = "free") +
   scale_x_discrete(limits = ANAM,
                    expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL,
       title = "",
       fill = "Value",
       color = "Value") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)) +
        guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 1,
                                     barwidth = 12,
                                   ticks = T,
                                   ticks.colour = "black",
                                   frame.colour = "black",
                                   frame.linewidth = 0.5,
                                   ticks.linewidth = 0.5),
               color = "none")
}
for(i in c(2:10, 12:17)) {
  FPLT[[i]] <- FPLT[[i]] + theme(strip.text.x.top = element_blank())
}
wrap_plots(FPLT[1:10], ncol = 1) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r batpcosummary2, fig.width=10, fig.height=10, message = F, warning=F}
wrap_plots(FPLT[11:17], ncol = 1) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

### Evaluation Metrics
```{r allvals, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, message = F, warning=F, fig.cap="A heatmap showing evaluation results for each model. In general, warmer colors relate to higher values (we want lower values for most metrics except for R^2) and darker colors represent lower values. Refer to the legend to see which metric is shown for each color scheme."}
VALS <- list(read.csv("ebov_mammphylo_11July2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("bdbv_mammphylo_16Aug2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("bomv_mammphylo_16July2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("tafv_mammphylo_19Aug2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("restv_mammphylo_22Aug2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("marv_mammphylo_08Jan2025_values.csv"),
             read.csv("llov_mammphylo_20Dec2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("sudv_mammphylo_23Aug2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("mlav_mammphylo_9Sep2024_values.csv"),
             read.csv("ravn_mammphylo_09Jan2025_values.csv"),
             read.csv("dehv_mammphylo_08Jul2025_values.csv")
)
for(i in 1:11) {
VALS[[i]] <- VALS[[i]] %>%
  filter(.metric == "rmse") %>%
  mutate(.estimate = .estimate/RANG[i],
         .metric = "rmse_perc") %>%
  rbind(VALS[[i]])
}
VIRN <- c("EBOV", "BDBV", "BOMV", "TAFV", "RESTV", "MARV", "LLOV", "SUDV", "MLAV", "RAVN", "DEHV")
VALS <- foreach(i = 1:length(VALS), .combine = "rbind") %do% {
        VALS[[i]] %>%
            group_by(.metric, type) %>%
            summarise(estimate = round(mean(.estimate), 3),
              sd_estimate = round(sd(.estimate), 3),
              min_95 = round(t.test(.estimate, conf.level = 0.95)$conf.int[1], 3),
              max_95 = round(t.test(.estimate, conf.level = 0.95)$conf.int[2], 3),
              min = round(min(.estimate), 3),
              max = round(max(.estimate), 3)) %>%
            data.frame() %>%
            mutate(virus = VIRN[i])
}
METR <- c("huber_loss", "mae", "rmse", "rmse_perc", "rsq")
VALP <- foreach(i = 1:5) %do% {
  valp <- VALS[VALS$.metric == METR[i], ] %>%
    ggplot() +
    geom_tile(aes(x = type,
                y = virus,
                fill = estimate),
            color = "black",
            linewidth = 0.4) +
    # shadowtext::geom_shadowtext(aes(x = type,
    #                     y = virus,
    #                     label = estimate),
    #                     size = 2.5) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(name = c("Huber loss", "MAE", "RMSE", "RMSE (%)", "R^2^")[i],
                         option = c("A", "B", "E", "G", "D")[i]) +
    scale_x_discrete(name = NULL,
                     limits = c("train", "test"),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_discrete(name = NULL,
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
    theme(aspect.ratio = 11/2,
          legend.title.position = "top",
          legend.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 1.2,
                                 barwidth = 10,
                                 direction = "horizontal", 
                                 # title.position = "top",
                                 # title.hjust = 0.5,
                                 ticks = T,
                                 ticks.colour = "black",
                                 frame.colour = "black"))
  if(i != 1) {
    valp + theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
                          axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                          axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  } else valp
}
wrap_plots(VALP, nrow = 1) + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

### Partial Dependence
```{r importancedata, message=F, results = "hide", warning=F}
SHAPS <- rbind(read.csv("ebov_mammphylo_11July2024_shap.csv") %>%
                #group_by(variable, row_index) %>%
                group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "ebov"),
              read.csv("bdbv_mammphylo_16Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
                group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "bdbv"),
              read.csv("bomv_mammphylo_16July2024_shap.csv") %>%
                group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "bomv"),
              read.csv("tafv_mammphylo_19Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "tafv"),
             read.csv("restv_mammphylo_22Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "restv"),
             read.csv("marv_mammphylo_08Jan2025_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "marv"),
             read.csv("llov_mammphylo_20Dec2024_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "llov"),
             read.csv("sudv_mammphylo_23Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, row_index) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "sudv"),
             read.csv("mlav_mammphylo_9Sep2024_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, row_index) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "mlav"),
             read.csv("ravn_mammphylo_09Jan2025_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                group_by(variable) %>%
                summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
                mutate(filo = "ravn"),
             read.csv("dehv_mammphylo_08Jul2025_shap.csv") %>%
               group_by(variable, iteration) %>%
               summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
               group_by(variable) %>%
               summarise(value = mean(abs(value), na.rm = T)) %>%
               mutate(filo = "dehv"))
```

```{r importanceall, warning=F}
GRP <- c("diet", "geography", "habitat", "movement", "phylogenetic", "reproduction", "size")
COLS <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
COLS <- cbind.data.frame(value = rev(COLS[1:7]),
                         name = c("diet", "geography", "habitat", "movement", "phylogenetic", "reproduction", "size"))
TABS <- SHAPS %>%
  mutate(variable = gsub("log_", "", variable)) %>%
  left_join(DICT, by = c("variable" = "var")) %>%
  select(variable, type) %>%
  distinct() %$%
  type %>%
  table()

LABS <- c("1-2", "2-3", "3-4", "4-5", "5-10", "10-15", "15-20", "> 20")
PLT1 <- SHAPS %>%
  mutate(variable = gsub("log_", "", variable)) %>%
  left_join(DICT, by = c("variable" = "var")) %>%
  mutate(value_cat = cut(value, c(seq(1, 5, 1), 10, 15, 20, 32), labels = LABS, include.lowest = T)) %>%
  mutate(type = case_when(type == "movement" ~ "move",
                          T ~ type)) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(x = variable, 
                y = filo,
                fill = type,
                alpha = value_cat),
            color = "black",
            linewidth = 0.3,
            show.legend = T) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90),
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0),
                   labels = c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "MLAV", "RAVV", "RESTV", "SUDV", "TAFV")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COLS[, 1]) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Filovirus",
       alpha = "mean |SHAP value|") +
  facet_grid(~type,
             scale = "free_x",
             space = "free",
             labeller = labeller(type = tools::toTitleCase)) +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white",
                                        linewidth = 0.8),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white",
                                        color = "black",
                                        linewidth = 0.8),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 12),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "transparent")) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  gghighlight(value_cat %in% c("1-2", "2-3", "3-4", "4-5", "5-10", "10-15", "15-20", "> 20"),
              unhighlighted_params = list(fill = "white",
                                          alpha = 1,
                                          color = "black",
                                          size = 0.1),
              calculate_per_facet = T)
#ggsave(plot = PLT1, "filoexample.png", units = "in", width = 16, height = 4)
```

```{r pdplotdata, warning=F, results="hide", message = F}
alt <- rbind(read.csv("ebov_mammphylo_11July2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "ebov"),
      read.csv("bdbv_mammphylo_16Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "bdbv"),
      read.csv("bomv_mammphylo_16July2024_shap.csv") %>%
      mutate(virus = "bomv"),
      read.csv("tafv_mammphylo_19Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "tafv"),
      read.csv("restv_mammphylo_22Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "restv"),
      read.csv("marv_mammphylo_08Jan2025_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "marv"),
      read.csv("llov_mammphylo_20Dec2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "llov"),
      read.csv("sudv_mammphylo_23Aug2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "sudv"),
      read.csv("mlav_mammphylo_9Sep2024_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "mlav"),
      read.csv("ravn_mammphylo_09Jan2025_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "ravn"),
      read.csv("dehv_mammphylo_08Jul2025_shap.csv") %>%
        mutate(virus = "dehv"))
alt %<>%
  group_by(variable, ID, row_index, rfvalue, virus) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = T),
            rfvalue = mean(rfvalue, na.rm = T)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  data.frame()
```

```{r densitytraining, warning = F, results = "hide", message = F}
DATA <- read.csv("mammphylodata_21Jan2025.csv")
RUGV <- DATA[, c("dphy_plant", "mean_aridity_index", "min_aridity_index", "upper_elevation_m", "aspect_ratio", "pco_17", "pco_4", "age_first_reproduction_d", "log_weaning_age_d", "relative_wing_loading")] %>%
    reshape2::melt() %>%
    rename(rfvalue = value) %>%
    mutate(value = 0)
TPLT <- foreach(i = 1:length(c("dphy_plant", "mean_aridity_index", "min_aridity_index", "upper_elevation_m", "pco_17", "pco_4", "age_first_reproduction_d", "log_weaning_age_d", "aspect_ratio", "relative_wing_loading"))) %do% {
    RUGV %>%
      filter(variable == c("dphy_plant", "mean_aridity_index", "min_aridity_index", "upper_elevation_m", "pco_17", "pco_4", "age_first_reproduction_d", "log_weaning_age_d", "aspect_ratio", "relative_wing_loading")[i]) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_density(aes(x = rfvalue),
                   fill = c("#A6761D", "#E6AB02", "#E6AB02", "#66A61E", "#7570B3", "#7570B3", "#D95F02", "#D95F02", "#1B9E77", "#1B9E77")[i],
                   alpha = 0.4,
                   color = c("#A6761D", "#E6AB02", "#E6AB02", "#66A61E", "#7570B3", "#7570B3", "#D95F02", "#D95F02", "#1B9E77", "#1B9E77")[i]) +
      scale_x_continuous(position = "bottom") +
      scale_y_reverse(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0))) +
      labs(x = c("Diet - plant", "Mean aridity index", "Min aridity index", "Upper elevation (m)", "PCo 17", "PCo 4", "Age at first reproduction (days)", "Weaning age (days)", "Aspect ratio", "Relative wing loading")[i],
           y = NULL) +
      theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white",
                                            color = "grey50"),
            panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey80"),
            panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "transparent"),
            panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                        color = "black"),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "transparent"),
            aspect.ratio = 0.2,
            plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"pt"))
}
```

```

### Training data - predictions

```{r treetrainingpredict, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap = "A phylogenetic tree of the bats included in our training and validation datasets with the predicted binding strength for each model shown as a heatmap to the right of the tree. Warmer colors depict higher binding strength and cooler colors show lower binding strengths."}
ATRE <- TREE
MISM <- GORK
#MISM$species[which(is.na(match(MISM$species, ATRE$tip.label)))] 
MISM %<>%
  mutate(species = case_when(species == "Unidentified Phyllostomid Bat" ~ NA,
                             species == "Unidentified Vespertillionid Bat" ~ NA,
                             species == "Scotophilus viridis nigritellus" ~ "Scotophilus viridis",
                             species == "Taphozous longimanes" ~ "Taphozous longimanus",
                             species == "Nycteris sp." ~ NA,
                             species == "Unidentified bat 1" ~ NA,
                             species == "Rhinolophus sp str1" ~ NA,
                             species == "Hipposideros ruber complex str2" ~ "Hipposideros ruber",
                             species == "Mormoops blainvillii" ~ "Mormoops blainvillei",
                             species == "Rhinolophus sp str 3" ~ NA,
                             species == "Rhinolophus sp str2" ~ NA,
                             species == "Artibeus glaucus" ~ "Dermanura glaucus",
                             species == "Mops cf nanulus" ~ "Mops nanulus",
                             species == "Neoromicia nanus" ~ "Neoromicia nana",
                             species == "Chaerephon pumilus str1" ~ "Chaerephon pumilus",
                             species == "Chaerephon pumilus str2" ~ "Chaerephon pumilus",
                             species == "Eptesicus fuscus wetmorei" ~ "Eptesicus guadeloupensis", #in Eptesicus dutertreus which includes this taxon
                             species == "Hipposideros ruber complex str1" ~ "Hipposideros ruber",
                             species == "Megaderma sp" ~ NA,
                             species == "Myopterus daubentoni" ~ "Myopterus daubentonii",
                             #species == "Pteronotus rubiginosus" ~ "Pteronotus parnellii",
                             species == "Pteronotus parnellii mesoamericanus" ~ "Pteronotus parnellii",
                             species == "Myonycteris angolensis smithii" ~ "Myonycteris angolensis",
                             species == "Unidentified Molossid Bat" ~ NA,
                             species == "Miniopterus fuliginosus" ~ "Miniopterus schreibersii",
                             species == "Pteropus dasymallus yayeyamae" ~ "Pteropus dasymallus",
                             species == "Pteropus sp" ~ NA,
                             species == "Myotis ricketti" ~ "Myotis pilosus",
                             species == "Epomophorus minor" ~ "Epomophorus minimus",
                             T ~ species))
#set.seed(1876)
MISM <- MISM[!is.na(MISM$species), ] %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise_all(mean, na.rm = T)
ATRE <- drop.tip(ATRE, which(!ATRE$tip.label %in% c(MISM$species)))
PVAL <- data.frame(BDBV = MISM$bdbv_auc, 
                   BOMV = MISM$bomv_auc,
                   DEHV = MISM$dehv_auc,
                   EBOV = MISM$ebov_auc,
                   LLOV = MISM$llov_auc,
                   MARV = MISM$marv_auc,
                   MLAV = MISM$mlav_auc,
                   RAVV = MISM$ravn_auc,
                   RESTV = MISM$restv_auc,
                   SUDV = MISM$sudv_auc,
                   TAFV = MISM$tafv_auc,
                   row.names = MISM$species)
PTRE <-  ggtree(ATRE,
                size = 0.08) +
  geom_tiplab(size = 1.5,
              align = T,
              linesize = 0.01,
              fontface = "italic") + 
    scale_x_ggtree() 
gheatmap(PTRE,
         PVAL,
         offset= 8.3,
         width=0.3,
         colnames = T,
         color = "black",
         colnames_angle=-90,
         hjust=0,
         font.size = 3) +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(10, 405),
                           breaks = seq(0, 400, 50),
                           option = "C") +
      labs(fill = "Binding Strength") +
      theme_void() +
      theme(#plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"null"),
            #panel.spacing = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "null"),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
            axis.text = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            axis.title = element_blank()) +
      guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 14,
                                   ticks = T,
                                   ticks.colour = "black",
                                   frame.colour = "black",
                                   frame.linewidth = 0.5,
                                   ticks.linewidth = 0.5)) +
  ggtree::vexpand(.1, -1)
#ggsave("FiloTrainingTree.png", units = "in", width = 8, height = 6)
```

### Training data - differences

```{r treetrainingdifference, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="A phylogenetic tree of species included in our training and validation datasets with a heatmap showing the difference between predicted and observed binding strength values. Blues represent predictions that are higher than observed values and reds show predictions that are lower than observed values. Darker shades show a greater distance between observed and predicted results."}
FRED <- read.csv("FilovirusPredictions.csv") 
FRED %<>%
  mutate(species = case_when(species == "Dermanura glauca" ~ "Dermanura glaucus",
                             species == "Gardnerycteris crenulatum" ~ "Mimon crenulatum",
                             species == "Lissonycteris angolensis" ~ "Myonycteris angolensis",
                             species == "Vampyriscus bidens" ~ "Vampyressa bidens",
                             T ~ species ))

FRED %<>%
  filter(species %in% MISM$species)
RISM <- MISM %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = 2:12) %>%
  mutate(name = case_when(name == "ebov_auc" ~ "EBOV",
                          name == "bomv_auc" ~ "BOMV",
                          name == "bdbv_auc" ~ "BDBV",
                          name == "tafv_auc" ~ "TAFV",
                          name == "restv_auc" ~ "RESTV",
                          name == "sudv_auc" ~ "SUDV",
                          name == "mlav_auc" ~ "MLAV",
                          name == "llov_auc" ~ "LLOV",
                          name == "marv_auc" ~ "MARV",
                          name == "ravn_auc" ~ "RAVN",
                          name == "dehv_auc" ~ "DEHV"))
FRED %<>%
  left_join(RISM, by = c("species", "virus" = "name")) %>%
  mutate(pred_diff = pred - value) %>%
  mutate(diff_cat = cut(pred_diff, c(-150, -100, -50, -25, -10, 10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 250)))
FRED %<>%
  dplyr::select(species, virus, diff_cat) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c("species"), names_from = "virus", values_from = "diff_cat") %>%
  data.frame()
FRED <- data.frame(FRED[, -1], row.names = FRED$species)
gheatmap(PTRE,
         FRED,
         offset= 8.3,
         width=0.3,
         colnames = T,
         color = "black",
         colnames_angle=-90,
         hjust=0,
         font.size = 3) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")[-1],
                        limits = levels(FRED$EBOV)) +
      labs(fill = "Binding Strength") +
      theme_void() +
      theme(#plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"null"),
            #panel.spacing = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "null"),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
            axis.text = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            axis.title = element_blank()) +
      #guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 14,
      #                             ticks = T,
      #                             ticks.colour = "black",
      #                             frame.colour = "black",
      #                             frame.linewidth = 0.5,
      #                             ticks.linewidth = 0.5)) +
  ggtree::vexpand(.1, -1)
```

### Training data - outlier species
```{r trainingabsdifference, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap= "A heatmap showing all species in the training and validation datasets that have a difference in predicted and observed binding strength greater than 50. All differences left unfilled are lower than 50, otherwise the scale shows warmer colors with larger differences and cooler colors with distances closer to 50."}
FRED <- read.csv("FilovirusPredictions.csv") 
FRED %<>%
  mutate(species = case_when(species == "Dermanura glauca" ~ "Dermanura glaucus",
                             species == "Gardnerycteris crenulatum" ~ "Mimon crenulatum",
                             species == "Lissonycteris angolensis" ~ "Myonycteris angolensis",
                             species == "Vampyriscus bidens" ~ "Vampyressa bidens",
                             T ~ species ))

FRED %<>%
  filter(species %in% MISM$species)
DRAN <- apply(MISM[, -1], 2, function(i) diff(range(i)) * 0.2)
names(DRAN) <- c("EBOV", "BDBV", "BOMV", "TAFV", "RESTV", "SUDV", "MARV", "LLOV", 
"MLAV", "RAVN", "DEHV")
RISM <- MISM %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = 2:12) %>%
  mutate(name = case_when(name == "ebov_auc" ~ "EBOV",
                          name == "bomv_auc" ~ "BOMV",
                          name == "bdbv_auc" ~ "BDBV",
                          name == "tafv_auc" ~ "TAFV",
                          name == "restv_auc" ~ "RESTV",
                          name == "sudv_auc" ~ "SUDV",
                          name == "mlav_auc" ~ "MLAV",
                          name == "llov_auc" ~ "LLOV",
                          name == "marv_auc" ~ "MARV",
                          name == "ravn_auc" ~ "RAVN",
                          name == "dehv_auc" ~ "DEHV"))
FRED %<>%
  left_join(RISM, by = c("species", "virus" = "name")) %>%
  mutate(pred_diff = abs(pred - value)) 
foreach(i = 1:length(DRAN), .combine = "rbind") %do% {
  FRED %>%
    filter(virus == names(DRAN)[i],
           pred_diff >= DRAN[i])
} %>%
  filter(!virus %in% c("MLAV", "SUDV")) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = "species", names_from = "virus", values_from = "pred_diff", values_fill = NA) %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(y = variable,
                x = species,
                fill = value),
            color = "black",
            linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 45), position = "top") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = "white",
                       name = "|Predicted - Observed|") +
  labs(x = "Species",
       y = "Virus") +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 9/28) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 10,
                              ticks = T,
                              ticks.colour = "black",
                              frame.colour = "black",
                              frame.linewidth = 0.5,
                              ticks.linewidth = 0.5))
```

### Predictions

```{r treepredict, warning = F, message = F, fig.cap="Phylogenetic trees of all bats with their predicted binding strengths shown around the tree. Warmer colors show higher predicted binding strengths and cooler colors show lower binding strengths. Note that some models have overall higher values and in others, clear phylogenetic patterns can be seen."}
ATRE <- TREE
FRED <- read.csv("FilovirusPredictions.csv") %>%
  filter(!virus %in% c("RESTV", "MLAV"))
#TREE$tip.label[which(is.na(match(TREE$tip.label, unique(FRED$species))))]
ATRE$tip.label[which(is.na(match(ATRE$tip.label, unique(FRED$species))))] <- c("Lyroderma lyra", "Macronycteris thomensis", "Macronycteris commersoni", "Macronycteris vittatus", "Macronycteris gigas", "Lissonycteris angolensis", "Scotonycteris ophiodon", "Boneia bidens", "Gardnerycteris koepckeae", "Lonchophylla pattoni", "Lonchophylla cadenai", "Dermanura tolteca", "Dermanura rosenbergi", "Dermanura azteca", "Dermanura glauca", "Dermanura cinerea", "Dermanura gnoma", "Vampyriscus bidens", "Vampyriscus brocki", "Vampyriscus nymphaea", "Gardnerycteris crenulatum", "Lophostoma occidentalis", "Paremballonura tiavato", "Paremballonura atrata", "Diclidurus isabella", "Austronomus kuboriensis", "Ozimops loriae", "Austronomus australis", "Baeodon gracilis", "Baeodon alleni", "Hypsugo macrotis", "Perimyotis subflavus", "Hypsugo vordermanni", "Hypsugo musciculus", "Hypsugo lophurus", "Hypsugo anthonyi", "Hypsugo eisentrauti", "Pipistrellus anchietae", "Hypsugo pulveratus", "Hypsugo affinis", "Hypsugo savii", "Hypsugo alaschanicus", "Hypsugo cadornae", "Hypsugo ariel", "Hypsugo arabicus", "Rhyneptesicus nasutus", "Hypsugo kitcheneri", "Hypsugo joffrei", "Murina lorelieae", "Murina feae")

PVAL <- lapply(unique(FRED$virus), function(i) {
    data.frame(FRED[FRED$virus == i, "pred"], row.names = FRED[FRED$virus == i, ]$species)
})
#PVAL <- pivot_wider(FRED, id_cols = "species", names_from = "virus", values_from = "pred")
#PVAL <- data.frame(PVAL[, -1], row.names = PVAL$species)
PTRE <- ggtree(ATRE,
               size = 0.05,
               layout = "circular",
               aes(angle = angle))
PPLT <- foreach(i = 1:length(PVAL)) %do% {
  gheatmap(PTRE, PVAL[[i]], offset= 0, width=0.1, colnames = F, color = NA) +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(50, 395),
                           breaks = seq(50, 400, 50),
                           option = "C") +
      labs(fill = "Binding Strength",
           title = unique(FRED$virus)[i]) +
      theme_void() +
      theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"null"),
            panel.spacing = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "null"),
            axis.text = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            axis.title = element_blank()) +
      guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 14,
                                   ticks = T,
                                   ticks.colour = "black",
                                   frame.colour = "black",
                                   frame.linewidth = 0.5,
                                   ticks.linewidth = 0.5))
}
wrap_plots(PPLT, nrow = 2) + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
#ggsave("FiloPhyloPredictions.png", units = "in", width = 10, height = 6)
```

```{r treepredictcat, eval = F}
PVL <- foreach(i = PVAL) %do% {
  cut(i[, 1], breaks = seq(0, 450, 75), include.lowest = T, labels = c("0 - 75", "76 - 150", "151 - 225", "226 - 300", "301 - 375", "376 - 450"))
}
for(i in 1:length(PVAL)) {
  PVAL[[i]][, 1] <- PVL[[i]]
}
PPLT <- foreach(i = 1:length(PVAL)) %do% {
  gheatmap(PTRE, PVAL[[i]], offset= 0, width=0.1, colnames = F, color = NA) +
      scale_fill_viridis_d(limits = c("0 - 75", "76 - 150", "151 - 225", "226 - 300", "301 - 375", "376 - 450"), drop = F) +
      labs(fill = NULL,
           title = unique(FRED$virus)[i]) +
      theme_void() +
      theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"null"),
            panel.spacing = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "null"),
            axis.text = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            axis.title = element_blank())
}

```

Binding strength is very useful, but it is difficult to view a span of colors and get clear patterns sometimes. So, we will apply a threshold for this exploratory figure (binding strength of Mops condylurus to BOMV) to get an idea of which species for which viruses have a higher binding strength (more so to explore which viruses have a ton of these species or a complete lack of them).
```{r treepredictbinary, warning = F, message= F, fig.cap="Applying a threshold of 217.9 binding strength to our predictions, these phylogenetic trees show in dark gray which groups of species show higher binding strength than our threshold."}
PVAL <- lapply(PVAL, function(i) i >= 217.8848)
PPLT <- foreach(i = 1:length(PVAL)) %do% {
  gheatmap(PTRE, PVAL[[i]], offset= 0, width=0.1, colnames = F, color = NA) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("white", "grey20")) +
      labs(fill = NULL,
           title = unique(FRED$virus)[i]) +
      theme_void() +
      theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0,"null"),
            panel.spacing = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "null"),
            axis.text = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
            axis.title = element_blank(),
            legend.position = "none") 
}
wrap_plots(PPLT, nrow = 2) + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
#ggsave("FiloPhyloPredictionsThreshold.png", units = "in", width = 10, height = 6)
```

### Prediction - family

```{r allfamilypredictions, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=15, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Panels showing the distribution of predicted binding strengths by family and virus. For each row, only species within that family are highlighted and each column represents a particular virus model. Predictions are arranged from highest to lowest with the color representing binding strength."}
ANAM <- lapply(1:9, function(i) {
  FRED %>%
  filter(virus == c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "RAVN", "SUDV", "TAFV")[i]) %>%
  arrange(desc(pred)) %$%
  species
})
FPLT <- foreach(i = 1:9) %do% {
  FRED %>%
  filter(family %in% c("Emballonuridae", "Hipposideridae", "Molossidae", "Miniopteridae", "Pteropodidae", "Rhinolophidae", "Phyllostomidae", "Vespertilionidae"),
         virus %in% c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "RAVN", "SUDV", "TAFV")[i]) %>%
  mutate(pred_cat = cut(pred, breaks = seq(0, 400, 50), include.lowest = T, labels = c("0 - 50", "50 - 100", "100 - 150", "150 - 200", "200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"))) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           aes(x = species,
               y = pred,
               color = pred_cat,
               fill = pred_cat),
           show.legend = T) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "B",
                       limits = c("0 - 50", "50 - 100", "100 - 150", "150 - 200", "200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"),
                       drop = F) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "B",
                        limits = c("0 - 50", "50 - 100", "100 - 150", "150 - 200", "200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"),
                        drop = F) +
  gghighlight(use_direct_label = F) +
  facet_wrap(~ family, 
             nrow = 8) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ANAM[[i]],
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Predicted Binding Strength",
       title = c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "RAVN", "SUDV", "TAFV")[i],
       fill = "Predicted\nBinding Strength",
       color = "Predicted\nBinding Strength") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)),
                     limits = c(0, 400)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "bottom")
}
for(i in 2:9) {
  FPLT[[i]] <- FPLT[[i]] + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                                 axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                                 axis.title.y = element_blank())
}
wrap_plots(FPLT, nrow = 1) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
#ggsave("AllFamilyFiloPreds.png", units = "in", width = 14, height = 9)
```

```{r allfamilypredictionsfiltered, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=15, fig.height=9, fig.cap="Here, we remove all predictions below 200 and show the same predictions to restrict to only higher binding strength species."}
FPLT <- foreach(i = 1:9) %do% {
  FRED %>%
  filter(family %in% c("Emballonuridae", "Hipposideridae", "Molossidae", "Miniopteridae", "Pteropodidae", "Rhinolophidae", "Phyllostomidae", "Vespertilionidae"),
         virus %in% c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "RAVN", "SUDV", "TAFV")[i]) %>%
  mutate(pred_cat = cut(pred, breaks = seq(200, 400, 50), include.lowest = T, labels = c("200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"))) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           aes(x = species,
               y = pred,
               color = pred_cat,
               fill = pred_cat),
           show.legend = T) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "C",
                       limits = c("200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"),
                       drop = F,
                       na.value = "grey75") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C",
                        limits = c("200 - 250", "250 - 300", "300 - 350", "350 - 400"),
                        drop = F,
                        na.value = "grey75") +
  gghighlight(use_direct_label = F) +
  facet_wrap(~ family, 
             nrow = 8) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ANAM[[i]],
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Predicted Binding Strength",
       title = c("BDBV", "BOMV", "DEHV", "EBOV", "LLOV", "MARV", "RAVN", "SUDV", "TAFV")[i],
       fill = "Predicted\nBinding Strength",
       color = "Predicted\nBinding Strength") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)),
                     limits = c(0, 400)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "bottom")
}
for(i in 2:9) {
  FPLT[[i]] <- FPLT[[i]] + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                                 axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                                 axis.title.y = element_blank())
}
wrap_plots(FPLT, nrow = 1) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
```{r ebovfampred, eval = F}
ANAM <- FRED %>%
  filter(virus == "EBOV") %>%
  arrange(desc(pred)) %$%
  species
FRED %>%
  filter(family %in% c("Emballonuridae", "Hipposideridae", "Molossidae", "Miniopteridae", "Pteropodidae", "Rhinolophidae", "Phyllostomidae", "Vespertilionidae"),
         virus %in% c("EBOV")) %>%

### Training Data - Gower Distance

```{r gowerdist, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=12, fig.height=11, fig.cap="As an way to explore meaningful outliers and how predictions clump amongst species, we show the Gower distances based on trait similarity across our training and validation species. The x-axis shows only training species, while the y-axis shows both training and validation dataset species."}
TRT <- read.csv("npc1prediction_20Mar2024.csv", row.names = 1) %>%
  filter(binomial %in% c(MISM$species, "Dermanura glauca", "Gardnerycteris crenulatum", "Lissonycteris angolensis", "Vampyriscus bidens", "Scotophilus viridis")) %>%
  mutate(relative_age_first_birth = age_first_reproduction_d/max_longevity_d,
         female_relative_maturity = female_maturity_d/max_longevity_d,
         postnatal_growth_rate = weaning_mass_g/neonate_mass_g,
         mass_specific_production_rate = (neonate_mass_g/adult_mass_g) * litter_size_n * litters_per_year_n)
TRNG <- read.csv("npc1training_18Mar2024.csv")
CLSK <- c("adult_mass_g", "brain_mass_g", "adult_body_length_mm", "adult_forearm_length_mm", "litter_size_n", "neonate_mass_g", "weaning_age_d", "weaning_mass_g", "lower_elevation_m", "median_aggregation_size", "mixed_species_association_n", "relative_age_first_birth", "female_relative_maturity")
for(i in CLSK) {
  if(min(TRT[, i], na.rm = T) < 0) {
  TRT[, i] <- TRT[, i] + abs(min(TRT[, i], na.rm = T))
}
  if(min(TRT[, i], na.rm = T) == 0) {
  TRT[, i] <- TRT[, i] + 0.1
}
  TRT[, i] <- log(TRT[, i])
}
colnames(TRT)[colnames(TRT) %in% CLSK] <- paste("log", colnames(TRT)[colnames(TRT) %in% CLSK], sep = "_")
TVAR <- c("log_adult_mass_g", "log_brain_mass_g", "log_adult_body_length_mm", "log_adult_forearm_length_mm", "max_longevity_d", "maturity_d", "female_maturity_d", "age_first_reproduction_d", "gestation_length_d", "log_litter_size_n", "litters_per_year_n", "interbirth_interval_d", "log_neonate_mass_g", "log_weaning_age_d", "generation_length_d", "hibernation_torpor", "dphy_invertebrate", "dphy_plant", "det_inv", "det_fruit", "det_nect", "det_diet_breadth_n", "trophic_level", "upper_elevation_m", "log_lower_elevation_m", "island_dwelling", "disected_by_mountains", "habitat_breadth_n", "foraging_aerial", "foraging_arboreal", "island_mainland", "island_endemic", "bio_afrotropical", "bio_indomalayan", "bio_nearctic", "bio_neotropical", "iucn_forest", "iucn_savanna", "iucn_shrubland", "iucn_grassland", "iucn_rocky", "iucn_caves", "iucn_artificialterr", "min_aridity_index", "mean_aridity_index", "perc80_aridity_index", "relative_wing_loading", "aspect_ratio", "log_median_aggregation_size", "migration", "cave_roost", "cavity_roost", "foliage_roost", "generalist_roost", "iucn_moistforest", "iucn_moistsavanna", "log_relative_age_first_birth", "log_female_relative_maturity", "mass_specific_production_rate")
LIM2 <- c("Ametrida centurio", "Anoura geoffroyi", "Artibeus concolor", "Artibeus jamaicensis", "Artibeus lituratus", "Balantiopteryx plicata", "Brachyphylla cavernarum", "Carollia brevicauda", "Carollia sowelli", "Centurio senex", "Chaerephon pumilus", "Chaerephon pumilus", "Coleura afra", "Cynopterus sphinx", "Dermanura glauca", "Desmodus rotundus", "Diaemus youngi", "Eidolon helvum", "Epomophorus labiatus", "Epomops buettikoferi", "Erophylla bombifrons", "Gardnerycteris crenulatum", "Glauconycteris poensis", "Glossophaga leachii", "Glossophaga soricina", "Hipposideros armiger", "Hipposideros jonesi", "Hipposideros larvatus", "Hipposideros ruber", "Hipposideros ruber", "Hypsignathus monstrosus", "Lissonycteris angolensis", "Macrotus waterhousii", "Micropteropus pusillus", "Molossops temminckii", "Molossus rufus", "Monophyllus redmani", "Mops condylurus", "Mormoops blainvillei", "Mormoops megalophylla", "Myotis bocagii", "Myotis brandtii", "Myotis davidii", "Myotis lucifugus", "Natalus stramineus", "Neoromicia nana", "Neoromicia rendalli", "Nycteris hispida", "Nyctinomops macrotis", "Phylloderma stenops", "Phyllostomus elongatus", "Phyllostomus hastatus", "Pipistrellus coromandra", "Pipistrellus nanulus", "Pteronotus quadridens", "Pteronotus rubiginosus", "Pteropus alecto", "Pteropus giganteus", "Rhinolophus hipposideros", "Rhinolophus shortridgei", "Rhinolophus sinicus", "Rhinophylla pumilio", "Rousettus leschenaultii", "Scotoecus albofuscus", "Scotophilus viridis", "Stenoderma rufum", "Sturnira lilium", "Sturnira luisi", "Taphozous longimanus", "Triaenops afer", "Uroderma bilobatum", "Vampyriscus bidens")
LIMS <- TRT %>%
  arrange(family) %$%
  binomial
LIM2 <- LIM2[na.omit(match(LIMS, LIM2))]
GOWD <- as.matrix(vegdist(TRT[, TVAR], method = "gower", na.rm = T)) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  mutate(species = TRT$binomial)
colnames(GOWD) <- c(TRT$binomial, "species")
GOWD %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(x = species,
                y = variable,
                fill = value),
            color = "black") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(LIM2),
                   guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = unique(LIMS)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL,
       fill = "Gower Distance") +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
   guides(fill = guide_colorbar(barheight = 10,
                                   ticks = T,
                                   ticks.colour = "black",
                                   frame.colour = "black",
                                   frame.linewidth = 0.5,
                                   ticks.linewidth = 0.5))
```

```{r gowerdist2, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=6, fig.height=11, fig.cap="To further explore, we show the range of Gower distances (x-axis) for each species (y-axis) with the color referencing the density of the distances seen across comparison with other species (warmer means higher density and darker means lower density). The panels separate species that show particularly poor predictions for at least one model (shown in the outlier plots)."}
GDEN <- lapply(1:nrow(GOWD), function(i) density(as.numeric(GOWD[i, LIM2]), from = 0, to = 0.625))
GDEN <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:length(GDEN), function(i) cbind.data.frame(x = GDEN[[i]]$x,
                                                                   y = GDEN[[i]]$y,
                                                                   species = GOWD$species[i]))) %>%
    mutate(outlier_status = case_when(species %in% c("Dermanura glauca", "Epomophorus minimus", "Gardnerycteris crenulatum", "Hipposideros diadema", "Hipposideros lekaguli", "Macroglossus sobrinus", "Miniopterus minor", "Miniopterus natalensis", "Miniopterus pusillus", "Miniopterus schreibersii", "Noctilio leporinus", "Pteronotus parnellii", "Rhinolophus ferrumequinum", "Vampyriscus bidens", "Epomops buettikoferi", "Lissonycteris angolensis", "Miniopterus magnater", "Mops condylurus", "Taphozous mauritianus", "Pipistrellus coromandra", "Rhinolophus shameli", "Eptesicus fuscus", "Kerivoula cuprosa", "Kerivoula smithii", "Murina leucogaster", "Carollia perspicillata", "Pipistrellus kuhlii", "Chaerephon nigeriae") ~ 1,
                                    T ~ 0))
MAXD <- do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(GDEN$species), function(i) GDEN[GDEN$species == i, ][which.max(GDEN[GDEN$species == i, "y"]), ]))
GDEN %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(x = x,
                y = species,
                fill = y)) +
  geom_point(data = MAXD,
             aes(x = x,
                 y = species)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  facet_grid(outlier_status ~., scales = "free_y", space = "free") +
      theme(strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r phydist, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=12, fig.height=11, fig.cap="As an way to explore meaningful outliers and how predictions clump amongst species, we show the branch length differences based on our phylogenetic trees across our training and validation species. The x-axis shows only training species, while the y-axis shows both training and validation dataset species."}
#load("~/Downloads/Filovirus/MammalPhyloDistance_1000trees.Rdata")
PHYD <- read.csv("batphylodist_09Jul2025.csv") %>%
  mutate(species = gsub("_", " ", colnames(.)))
colnames(PHYD) <- gsub("_", " ", colnames(PHYD))
reshape2::melt(PHYD) %>%
  filter(variable %in% LIM2) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(x = variable,
                y = species,
                fill = value),
            color = "black") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D",
                       na.value = "grey50") +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90),
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
    theme(strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
        legend.position = "right")
```

```{r phydist2, warning=F, message = F, fig.width=6, fig.height=11, fig.cap="To further explore, we show the range of branch length distances (x-axis) for each species (y-axis) with the color referencing the density of the distances seen across comparison with other species (warmer means higher density and darker means lower density). The panels separate species that show particularly poor predictions for at least one model (shown in the outlier plots)."}
PDEN <- lapply(1:nrow(PHYD), function(i) density(as.numeric(PHYD[i, LIM2[!is.na(match(LIM2, colnames(PHYD)))]]), from = 0, to = 134.1))
PDEN <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:length(PDEN), function(i) cbind.data.frame(x = PDEN[[i]]$x,
                                                                   y = PDEN[[i]]$y,
                                                                   species = PHYD$species[i]))) %>%
    mutate(outlier_status = case_when(species %in% c("Dermanura glauca", "Epomophorus minimus", "Gardnerycteris crenulatum", "Hipposideros diadema", "Hipposideros lekaguli", "Macroglossus sobrinus", "Miniopterus minor", "Miniopterus natalensis", "Miniopterus pusillus", "Miniopterus schreibersii", "Noctilio leporinus", "Pteronotus parnellii", "Rhinolophus ferrumequinum", "Vampyriscus bidens", "Epomops buettikoferi", "Lissonycteris angolensis", "Miniopterus magnater", "Mops condylurus", "Taphozous mauritianus", "Pipistrellus coromandra", "Rhinolophus shameli", "Eptesicus fuscus", "Kerivoula cuprosa", "Kerivoula smithii", "Murina leucogaster", "Carollia perspicillata", "Pipistrellus kuhlii", "Chaerephon nigeriae") ~ 1,
                                    T ~ 0))
MAXD <- do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(PDEN$species), function(i) PDEN[PDEN$species == i, ][which.max(PDEN[PDEN$species == i, "y"]), ]))
PDEN %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_tile(aes(x = x,
                y = species,
                fill = y)) +
  geom_point(data = MAXD,
             aes(x = x,
                 y = species)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  facet_grid(outlier_status ~., scales = "free_y", space = "free") +
      theme(strip.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "seashell"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
                                    color = "black"),
        panel.background = element_rect(color = "black",
                                        fill = "white"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```
